CREATE TABLE test_timestamps (id INT, event_time TIMESTAMP);
SELECT event_time, last_day(event_time) AS last_day FROM test_timestamps;
SELECT t.id, last_day(t.event_time) AS end_of_month FROM test_timestamps t WHERE t.event_time < TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01';
CREATE TABLE events (event_id INT, start_time TIMESTAMP, end_time TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO events VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-12-01 10:00:00', TIMESTAMP '2023-12-03 15:00:00');
INSERT INTO events VALUES (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-08-05 09:00:00', TIMESTAMP '2023-08-05 17:00:00');
SELECT e.event_id, last_day(e.start_time) AS last_day_start FROM events e;
SELECT e.event_id, last_day(e.end_time) AS last_day_end FROM events e;
SELECT e.event_id, last_day(e.start_time) AS last_day_start, last_day(e.end_time) AS last_day_end FROM events e WHERE last_day(e.start_time) > TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01';
SELECT e.event_id, last_day(e.start_time) AS last_day_start FROM events e JOIN test_timestamps t ON e.start_time > t.event_time;
SELECT event_id, last_day(start_time + interval '1 day') AS adjusted_last_day FROM events;
